page.content: In the analysis of the impact of oligomenorrhoea on patient outcomes, the data presented in the accompanying table offers critical insights into the relationship between various classifications, specifically when cross-classified with the variables of Volumetric Fat Area (VFA) and Subcutaneous Fat Area (SFA). The hazard ratios (HR) indicate significant differences in outcomes, with notably lower p-values, particularly the p-value of <0.0001 associated with local outcomes, suggesting a strong correlation between these fat classifications and the incidence of events.

page.content: Furthermore, the table delineates the distribution of cases, categorized as local, distant, or unknown, providing a comprehensive overview of the affected populations. For instance, the data shows that out of the total cohort, 18 individuals were classified under oligomenorrhoea, with 17 (94.4%) presenting local outcomes. These findings underscore the necessity for further investigation into how fat distribution may influence clinical outcomes in this demographic, thereby informing treatment protocols and patient management strategies.
